questions.question: Which two signs should be interchanged to make the given equation correct? 588÷28×32+72-160=760
questions.explanation: On interchanging – and + sign [option D] =588÷28×32-72+160 =21×32-72+160 =672+88 =760 Hence, option D is correct.

questions.question: Where was the Indian National Congress session of 1934 presided by Dr Rajendra Prasad? > डॉ. राजेंद्र प्रसाद की अध्यक्षता में 1934 का भारतीय राष्ट्रीय कांग्रेस अधिवेशन कहाँ हुआ था?
questions.explanation: The Bombay session was conducted under the presidency of Dr Rajendra Prasad. Jawaharlal Nehru moved the resolution for convening a Constituent Assembly to frame a suitable Constitution for free India at this session. Rajendra Prasad served as the first President of India, holding the office from January 26, 1950, until May 13, 1962. > बॉम्बे सत्र डॉ राजेंद्र प्रसाद की अध्यक्षता में आयोजित किया गया था। जवाहरलाल नेहरू ने इस सत्र में स्वतंत्र भारत के लिए एक उपयुक्त संविधान बनाने के लिए एक संविधान सभा बुलाने का प्रस्ताव पेश किया। राजेंद्र प्रसाद ने भारत के पहले राष्ट्रपति के रूप में कार्य किया और 26 जनवरी, 1950 से 13 मई, 1962 तक कार्यालय संभाला।

questions.question: Match the following: Dance State A) Dhimsa 1) Kerala B) Elelakkaradi 2) Manipur C) Waghya Murali 3) Maharashtra D) Pung Cholom 4) Andhra Pradesh
questions.explanation: • Dhimsa is a tribal dance form that is performed primarily by Porja caste women in Andhra Pradesh. • The Elelakkaradi dance is mainly performed in the state of Kerala, India। It is a tribal dance performed by the Kurichiya and Irular tribes. The dance is performed by men, women, and children. The dance is performed to the beat of drums called chenda and thappu. The dance is traditionally performed during the festival of Onam. • Waghya Murali is a traditional folk dance from Maharashtra, India, deeply rooted in religious and cultural practices. It is associated with the worship of Lord Khandoba, a regional deity revered as an incarnation of Lord Shiva. • Pung Cholom is a mesmerizing traditional dance form from Manipur, India. It is also known as the "Drum Dance" because performers play the Pung [a traditional Manipuri drum] while dancing.

questions.question: World’s first high-altitude Para Sports Centre will be built in:
questions.explanation: The world’s first high-altitude Para Sports Centre will be built in Leh, Ladakh. This initiative is collaboration between the Ladakh Autonomous Hill Development Council [LAHDC] and the Aditya Mehta Foundation [AMF]. The center aims to provide specialized training for para-athletes, helping them prepare for international competitions like the Paralympics.

questions.question: Krishna III of Manyakheta belonged to which dynasty?
questions.explanation: Krishna III of Manyakheta Ruled from 939–967 CE belonged to the Rashtrakuta dynasty. He was the last great emperor of the Rashtrakuta dynasty. He was a skilled military campaigner who fought many wars to restore the Rashtrakuta Empire and a shrewd administrator who ruled over a vast empire. His empire stretched from the Narmada River in the north to the Kaveri river delta in the south. Dantidurga [735–756 CE] Dantidurga defeated the Chalukyan king, Kirtivarman II, and established the Rashtrakuta dynasty. He was the founder of the Rashtrakuta Empire who fixed his capital at Manyakheta or Malkhed near modern Sholapur.

questions.question: Bhogali Bihu is a famous festival of Assam celebrated in which of the following month?
questions.explanation: Bhogali Bihu, also known as Magh Bihu, is celebrated in the month of January in Assam. Bhogali Bihu marks the end of the harvest season and is considered a celebration of abundance, hence the name “Bhogali” which means “feasting”. Other Bihu festival of State Assam:- There are three Bihu festival namely ‘Bohag Bihu’ celebrated in the month of ‘Bohag’ [Baisakh, the middle of April], ‘Magh Bihu’ celebrated in the month of ‘Magh’ [the middle of January], and ‘Kati Bihu’ celebrated in the month of ‘Kati’ [Kartik, the middle of October].

questions.question: Hypersonic speed is conveniently defined as any speed above______?
questions.explanation: Hypersonic speed is conveniently defined as any speed above Mach 5 [five times the speed of sound]. At this velocity, objects travel at approximately 6,174 kilometers per hour [3,836 miles per hour] or more, depending on the altitude and atmospheric conditions.

questions.question: Which of the following dam is located in Madhya Pradesh?
questions.explanation: • The Tawa Dam is located in Madhya Pradesh. The Tawa Dam is situated on the Tawa River in the Hoshangabad district of Madhya Pradesh. • Ujani Dam, also known as Bhima Dam or Bhima Irrigation Project, on the Bhima River, a tributary of the Krishna River. • Isapur Dam, which is an impressive earthfill dam located on the Penganga River near Kalamnuri in Maharashtra, India. • The Koyna Dam is one of the largest dams in Maharashtra, India. It is a rubble-concrete dam built on the Koyna River, which originates in Mahabaleshwar, a hill station in the Western Ghats.

questions.question: Which of the following is not a product made from Crude oil?
questions.explanation: Molasses is a viscous by-product, principally obtained from the refining of sugarcane or sugar beet juice into sugar. Molasses is rich in vitamins and minerals, including vitamin B6, iron, calcium, magnesium, and potassium. By Products of Crude Oil: - Paraffin wax, Kerosene, Lubricating oil, Gasoline, Diesel fuel, Asphalt, Bitumen, Naphtha.

questions.question: What was the child sex ratio in India according to the 2011 Census?
questions.explanation: According to the 2011 census, the child sex ratio in India was 919 females per 1,000 males. This was a decline from 927 in 2001. The child sex ratio is the number of females per 1,000 males in the age group of 0-6 years. It’s a key indicator of gender equality and the status of girls in a society. A low child sex ratio can indicate gender-based discrimination. Arunachal Pradesh has the highest child sex ratio among the Indian states i.e.972 per thousand males. Haryana has the lowest child sex ratio i.e. 834 per thousand males.

questions.question: On which date the “World Day against Child Labour” is Celebrated?
questions.explanation: The "World Day against Child Labour" is observed every year on June 12th. This day raises awareness and calls for action to prevent child labor, ensuring children have access to education and a better future.

questions.question: The Executive power of the Union is vested in the ________?
questions.explanation: The Executive power of the Union is vested in the President of India as per Article 53 of the Indian Constitution. The President exercises these powers directly or through officers subordinate to them, in accordance with the Constitution. While the Prime Minister is the real head of the executive branch and the chief decision-maker in practice. The Prime Minister leads the Council of Ministers, which collectively exercises executive authority on behalf of the President, as outlined in the Constitution. The Comptroller and Auditor General of India [CAG] is an independent constitutional authority established under Article 148 of the Indian Constitution. The CAG is responsible for auditing the accounts of the Union and State governments and ensuring transparency and accountability in the management of public funds. The CAG reports to the President of India, who lays these reports before Parliament.

questions.question: Who give the energy transmission rule from one trophic level to another in a food chain?
questions.explanation: The 10% Rule of energy transmission in a food chain was proposed by Raymond Lindeman in 1942. This rule states that, on average, only about 10% of the energy at one trophic level is transferred to the next trophic level, while the remaining 90% is lost, primarily as heat through metabolic processes. Ernst Haeckel, a renowned German zoologist, naturalist, and artist gave many biological terms, such as ecology, phylogeny, and Protista. Sir Arthur George Tansley was a pioneering English botanist and ecologist. He is best known for introducing the concept of ‘the ecosystem’ into biology, which revolutionized the study of ecological systems. The Five Kingdom Classification was proposed by R.H. Whittaker in 1969. This system categorizes all living organisms into five kingdoms: Monera, Protista, Fungi, Plantae, and Animalia. It was based on criteria such as cell structure, mode of nutrition, reproduction, and phylogenetic relationships.

questions.question: Angiosperms are divided into ________ groups on the basis of the no of Cotyledans present in the seed.
questions.explanation: Angiosperms are divided into two groups based on the number of cotyledons [seed leaves] present in the seed: 1. Monocotyledons [Monocots]: These have one cotyledon in their seeds [e.g., wheat, rice, and maize]. 2. Dicotyledons [Dicots]: These have two cotyledons in their seeds [e.g., beans, mango, and peas].

questions.question: What is the common name for the chemical formula K₂SO₄[Al₂[SO₄]₂]·24H₂O?
questions.explanation: The common name for the chemical compound K₂SO₄[Al₂[SO₄]₂]·24H₂O is Potassium Alum or simply Potash Alum. It is a widely used type of alum and is commonly found in water purification, leather tanning, and as an astringent in medicinal applications. It's also known as “Phitkari." Chemical Formula for Potassium Sulphate is K₂SO₄ and it is a widely used inorganic fertilizer. Chemical formula for Potassium Nitrate is KNO₃. It is a naturally occurring mineral also known as saltpetre or niter. Potassium nitrate is widely used for various purposes, including: Fertilizer, Food preservation, Pyrotechnics and explosives etc. Aluminium Sulphate, with the chemical formula Al₂[SO₄]₃, is a white, crystalline compound primarily used in industrial and water treatment processes. It’s utilized as Water Purification, Paper Industry, Dyeing Textiles, and Lowers soil pH.

questions.question: Who is the author of the book ‘Defending India’?
questions.explanation: ‘Defending India’ a master piece written by Jaswant Singh, Deputy Chairman, Planning Commission, in the Indian cabinet. This book attempts to comprehensively analyse the management of conflicts and security challenges faced by India during its first half century as a free country. Some famous books of Khushvant Singh are Truth, Love and a Little Malice: An Autobiography, 2002; Ghadar 1915: India’s first armed revolution, 1966; The Mark of Vishnu and Other Stories, [short story collection] 1950; The History of Sikhs, 1953; Train to Pakistan, [novel] 1956.

questions.question: Who defeated an Afghan ruler named Sultan Muhammad Ghori in 1191?
questions.explanation: Prithviraj Chauhan, also known as Prithviraj III, defeated Sultan Muhammad Ghori in the First Battle of Tarain in 1191. Prithviraj was a Rajput warrior king from the Chauhan dynasty. He was known for his military skills, including the “Shabhbhedi” technique of aiming at a target based on its sound. Muhammad Ghori was the ruler of the Ghurid Empire. He returned to Khurasan and came back a year later with a larger army. He defeated Prithviraj in the Second Battle of Tarain in 1192. This victory led to the establishment of Muslim rule in India.

questions.question: Which of the following is not a metalloid?
questions.explanation: Gallium [Ga] is a soft, silvery metal with an atomic number of 31. Metalloids are elements that exhibit properties intermediate between metals and nonmetals. They are located along the "stair-step" line on the periodic table, which divides metals and nonmetals. The six commonly recognized metalloids are: 1. Boron [B], 2. Silicon [Si], 3. Germanium [Ge], 4. Arsenic [As], 5. Antimony [Sb], 6. Tellurium [Te].

questions.question: The objective ‘to double the Per Capita Income of India’ within the next ten years was related to which of the following five year plan?
questions.explanation: The objective to double the per capita income within a decade was a key focus of the Tenth Five-Year Plan [2002–2007] in India. This plan aimed to achieve faster economic growth and improve living standards by targeting an 8% annual GDP growth rate and addressing poverty reduction and employment generation. Plan Holidays were a unique concept in India's economic planning history, referring to the period between 1966 and 1969. During this time, the government did not formally implement a Five-Year Plan. Instead, annual plans were introduced to address immediate concerns, as the country faced several challenges, including severe droughts, food shortages, and economic instability after the Indo-Pak war of 1965. The Fourth Five-Year Plan was launched in 1969. It had a dual focus: achieving economic stability and boosting agricultural production. Rolling Plan was introduced in India during 1978–1980 by the Janata Party government, replacing the earlier Five-Year Plans. The Rolling Plan approach was dynamic and flexible, as it allowed for annual revisions based on the nation's changing needs and economic conditions. It essentially comprised three types of plans: 1. A short-term plan for the current year. 2. A medium-term plan for a fixed period [usually three to five years]. 3. A long-term perspective plan for a longer horizon. This method was intended to adapt to uncertainties and avoid rigid targets. However, the Rolling Plan was discontinued when the Congress government returned to power in 1980, reinstating the traditional Five-Year Plans.

questions.question: Who among the following is appointed Director General of SSB?
questions.explanation: The current Director General of the Sashastra Seema Bal [SSB] is Amrit Mohan Prasad, an IPS officer from the Odisha cadre. He took over this role in 2024 and has extensive experience in law enforcement and security. The Sashastra Seema Bal [SSB] works under the Ministry of Home Affairs. It was established in 1963 in the aftermath of the Sino-Indian War to strengthen India's border areas. The SSB is primarily responsible for guarding India's borders with Nepal and Bhutan.

questions.question: The workforce population includes people aged from ____years to ____ years.
questions.explanation: The workforce population typically includes people aged from 15 years to 59 years. It generally represents the age group that is considered capable of working, excluding children and the elderly who are retired or less likely to participate in the labour force. The population of a nation is generally grouped into three broad categories: Children [generally below 15 years], Working Age [15-59 years] and Aged [Above 59 years].

questions.question: Which National Park is located in the south of Garo hills in Meghalaya?
questions.explanation: Balpakram National Park, located in the southern part of the Garo Hills in Meghalaya, is a protected area known for its rich biodiversity. It is home to several rare and endangered species of flora and fauna, including Bengal tigers, red pandas, wild water buffalo, and hoolock gibbons. The park also hosts unique plant species such as ground orchids and Schima wallichii trees. In addition, Balpakram serves as an important nesting ground for both migratory and resident bird species, making it a haven for wildlife enthusiasts and researchers alike.

questions.question: Which of the following is wrong statement about La-Nina?
options: Increased cloudiness and rainfall over Indonesia
Occurs when the ocean surface in the central and eastern Pacific Ocean becomes cooler than normal.
A climate event that can cause more rainfall and lower temperatures in India.
questions.explanation: La Nina means the Little Girl in Spanish. It is also sometimes called anti-El Nino, or simply “a cold event”. La Nina events represent periods of below-average sea surface temperatures across the east-central Equatorial Pacific. La Nina event is observed when the water temperature in the Eastern Pacific gets comparatively colder than normal, as a consequence of which, there is a strong high pressure over the eastern equatorial Pacific and causes stronger easterly trade winds. La Niña can be beneficial for India because it can lead to a good monsoon, which can help agriculture and water supplies. However, excessive rainfall can also cause flooding and crop damage.

questions.question: Which of the following article is related to the use of language in Parliament?
questions.explanation: • Article 120 of the Indian Constitution governs the language used in Parliament and states that business in Parliament shall be conducted in Hindi or English. However, members who cannot adequately express themselves in either language may seek permission to speak in their mother tongue. • Article 112 of the Indian Constitution relates to the Annual Financial Statement, commonly referred to as the Union Budget. It mandates the President of India to lay before both Houses of Parliament the estimated receipts and expenditures of the government for the coming financial year. This document provides a comprehensive account of the government's financial plans, policies, and allocations. • Article 110 of the Indian Constitution defines a Money Bill. The authority to determine whether a bill is a Money Bill lies with the Speaker of the Lok Sabha. Money Bills can only be introduced in the Lok Sabha, and the Rajya Sabha has limited powers regarding them, such as making recommendations but not amending or rejecting the bill outright. • Article 108 of the Indian Constitution provides for a joint sitting of both Houses of Parliament to resolve any deadlock over a bill. The President of India summons the joint sitting, and the session is presided over by the Speaker of the Lok Sabha. It's important to note that Money Bills and Constitutional Amendment Bills are not subjected to this procedure.

questions.question: Who among the following was a Portuguese writer who wrote about the trade and society in South India? > निम्नलिखित में से कौन एक पुर्तगाली लेखक था जिसने दक्षिण भारत में व्यापार और समाज के बारे में लिखा था?
questions.explanation: The Portuguese writer who wrote about trade and society in South India was Duarte Barbosa. Duarte Barbosa was a Portuguese officer stationed in India who provided detailed accounts of the trade and social structure of southern India in his writings. The Portuguese travellers Domingo Paes and Duarte Barbosa visited Vijayanagara during the time of Krishna Deva Raya. > दक्षिण भारत में व्यापार और समाज के बारे में लिखने वाले पुर्तगाली लेखक दुआरते बारबोसा थे। दुआरते बारबोसा भारत में तैनात एक पुर्तगाली अधिकारी थे जिन्होंने अपने लेखन में दक्षिण भारत के व्यापार और सामाजिक संरचना का विस्तृत विवरण प्रदान किया था। पुर्तगाली यात्री डोमिंगो पेस और दुआर्ते बारबोसा ने कृष्ण देव राय के समय विजयनगर का दौरा किया था।

questions.question: Which State Govt. has given a positive nod for revamping the Dulari Kanya Scheme?
questions.explanation: The Arunachal Pradesh Government has given a positive nod to revamp the Dulari Kanya Scheme. The fixed deposit amount for institutional births of girl children has been increased from ₹20,000 to ₹30,000, and an additional ₹20,000 is provided for Class XI admission through direct benefit transfer. This initiative aims to promote institutional births and support girls' education.

questions.question: A circle touches all the four sides of quadrilateral ABCD whose sides are AB = 8.4 cm, BC = 9.8 cm, and CD = 5.6 cm. The what is the length of side AD [in cm]?
questions.explanation: Formula: AB+CD=BC+AD AD + 9.8 = 5.6 + 8.4 AD = 4.2 cm

The table below shows the sales of salesman A as a percentage of the total sale of milk in each state. The total sale of milk is 200,000 litres. ![](https://www.thepunditsofficial.in/admin/uploads/questions/q_6997052a632d86.87165290.png) What will be the difference [in litres] in the sale of milk in state T by salesman B and the total sale of milk in states R and S together?
questions.explanation: t = 200000 T by B = 22%×40%×t = 17600 Total sale in R & S together = [17%+13%]×t =60000 Diff = 42400

questions.question: The following sentence has been split into four segments. Identify the segment that contains a grammatical error.
questions.explanation: The error lies in segment "are to be maintained". CONCEPT: • "Law and order" is often treated as a singular entity when referring to the concept of maintaining societal rules. Even though "Law and order" consists of two nouns, it is commonly considered singular in this context. • Therefore, the verb "is" should be used instead of "are." Here are some more examples for better understanding: • Bullock and cart is still used in some rural areas. • Bread and butter is my favourite breakfast. Correct Sentence: "Law and order is to be maintained at all costs."

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank. The work was _________ by the rains.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Hampered Explanation: The word "hampered" is the most appropriate choice to fill the blank in this sentence because it implies that the work was delayed or made more difficult due to the rains. It suggests that the rains created difficulties, but the work wasn't necessarily fully stopped. Here are the meanings and examples of all options: • Hampered [Correct] – बाधित किया गया – To prevent or delay the progress of something. Example: "The heavy snowfall hampered the progress of the construction work." • Restricted – सीमित – To limit or control the amount of something. Example: "Access to the building was restricted after the accident." • Obstructed – अवरुद्ध – To block or get in the way of something, usually referring to physical obstacles. Example: "The fallen tree obstructed the road." • Prevented – रोका – To stop something from happening entirely. Example: "The accident prevented the driver from continuing the journey."

questions.question: Select the option that will improve the underlined part of the given sentence. In case no improvement is needed, select 'No improvement required'. We spent an hour discussing about his character.
questions.explanation: Correct Answer: His character Explanation: In English, when the verb "discuss" is used, it is not followed by "about." The correct form is simply "discuss something i.e. discussing his character." Corrected sentence: "We spent an hour discussing his character."

questions.question: Select the Grammatically correct sentence -
options: I did not see him since he left college.
I could not see him since he left college.
I shall not see him since he left college.
I have not seen him since he left college.
questions.explanation: Correct Answer: I have not seen him since he left college. Explanation: • Present Perfect Tense: When you refer to an action that started in the past and continues to the present, you use the Present Perfect Tense. The sentence indicates that the action of "seeing" him did not occur after a specific point in the past [the time he left college], which is why "I have not seen" is the correct usage here. • The Present Perfect Tense is used when referring to an action or event that happened at an unspecified time before now and has relevance to the present moment. • Structure: Subject + has/have + past participle [e.g., have seen, has don]. Corrected sentence: "I have not seen him since he left college."

questions.question: Identify the word that is correctly spelt.
questions.explanation: The correct spelling is "Autumn." Here are the correct spellings and meanings of all options: • Autumn [Correct]– शरद ऋतु – The season between summer and winter, typically characterized by falling leaves and cooler temperatures. Example: "The autumn leaves are falling from the trees." • Condmn [Incorrect] → Condemn [Correct] – दोषी ठहराना – To express strong disapproval or to officially declare something as wrong. Example: "The judge will condemn the criminal for his actions." • Symptm [Incorrect] → Symptom [Correct] – लक्षण – A sign or indication of something, often related to illness. Example: "The fever was a symptom of the flu." • Columen [Incorrect] → Column [Correct] – स्तंभ – A vertical division or structure, or a regular article or feature in a newspaper or magazine. Example: "The newspaper has a weekly column on current events."

questions.question: Select the option that will improve the underlined part of the given sentence. In case no improvement is needed, select 'No improvement required'. I had to lay out running for several months.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Lay off Explanation: In this sentence, the phrase "lay off" is the most appropriate. "Lay off" means to stop or pause doing something temporarily, which fits with the idea of having to stop running for several months. Here are the meanings and examples of all options: • Lay away - संचय करना[ – To save something for future use, often by putting it aside for later. Example: "I will lay away some money for the vacation next year." • Lay by - संग्रह करना – To save or set aside for future use, similar to "lay away". Example: "He laid by enough money for his retirement." • Lay off - रुकना – To stop or discontinue something temporarily, especially work or an activity. Example: "The coach told the players to lay off training for a few days due to injury." • Lay down - बिछाना – To place something on a surface or to set something down. Example: "He laid down his book on the table." Corrected sentence: "I had to lay off running for several months."

questions.question: Select the most appropriate synonym of the underlined word in the following sentence - We received SANCTION to proceed with our plans.
questions.explanation: Sol: Correct Answer: Approval Explanation: The word "sanction" means official permission or approval to do something. The most appropriate synonym for "sanction" in this context is "approval." Let's see the meaning of all the options: • Sanction - स्वीकृति – Official permission or approval. Example: "The project was approved after receiving sanction." • Approval - स्वीकृति – The belief or acceptance that something is good or acceptable. Example: "We received approval for our plans." • Footprints - पदचिह्न – Marks left by feet or an indication of movement. Example: "The footprints led to the hidden treasure." • Charity - दान – The voluntary giving of help, usually in money, to those in need. Example: "The charity event raised significant funds." • Support - समर्थन – Assistance or help provided to someone. Example: "He offered his support during tough times." Corrected Sentence: "We received approval to proceed with our plans."

questions.question: Select the most appropriate antonym of the given word. FECUND
questions.explanation: Correct Answer: Barren Explanation: The word "fecund" refers to being fertile, fruitful, or capable of producing abundant offspring or growth. The opposite of fecund, which implies richness or abundance, is "barren," meaning incapable of producing or lacking in growth or fertility. Let's see the meaning of all the options along with their usage: • Fecund – उपजाऊ / फलदार – Refers to the ability to produce abundantly, often in terms of offspring or growth. Example: "The fecund soil produced a rich harvest every year." • Barren – बंजर / निष्क्रिय – Refers to being incapable of producing offspring or growth, the direct opposite of fecund. Example: "The barren land yielded no crops despite the efforts." • Strange – अजीब / विचित्र – Refers to something unfamiliar or odd, not related to fertility or productivity. Example: "It was strange to hear that news from a friend." • Radiant – दीप्तिमान / चमकदार – Refers to something that emits light or is glowing, not related to fertility or productivity. Example: "Her radiant smile lit up the room." • Familiar – परिचित – Refers to something known or recognized, not related to fertility or growth. Example: "The familiar sound of the wind calmed him."

questions.question: Choose the most appropriate option to make the sentence sensibly correct. He is a descendant ______ the Mughal royalty.
questions.explanation: Correct Answer: Of Explanation: The correct preposition to use with "descendant" when referring to someone's lineage is "of." The phrase "descendant of" indicates that someone is descended from a particular group, family, or royalty. Key Difference: • Of is used to indicate belonging or origin from a group, family, or lineage. Example: "He is a descendant of the Mughal royalty." • From is used to indicate the place of origin, but not specifically in reference to lineage. Example: "She is from New York." • For and In are not used in the context of being a descendant of something. Corrected Sentence: "He is a descendant of the Mughal royalty."

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning for the given idiom. Want to curl up and die
options: Extremely exhausted and need some rest
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Feeling terribly ashamed and sorry. Explanation: The idiom "want to curl up and die" refers to a feeling of deep embarrassment, shame, or guilt. It expresses the overwhelming desire to hide or withdraw from a situation due to these strong negative emotions. The person feels so ashamed or regretful that they figuratively want to "curl up" and escape from the world. Origin of the Idiom: This idiom originates from the idea of curling up, which conveys the image of retreating or withdrawing into oneself as a response to intense emotional discomfort or humiliation. It is often used when a person feels regret or is embarrassed by something they have said or done. Example: "After making that embarrassing mistake in front of her friends, she wanted to curl up and die." Hindi – उसने अपनी दोस्त के सामने गलती से कुछ कहा, और अब वह शर्म से मरना चाहती थी।] Therefore, the correct meaning of the idiom "want to curl up and die" is "feeling terribly ashamed and sorry."

questions.question: Identify the segment in the sentence that contains grammatical error.
questions.explanation: Error in segment: Whom did you CONCEPT: • The error lies in the incorrect use of "whom" in the sentence. In English, "whom" is typically used when it is the object of the verb or preposition, whereas "who" is the correct word when it is the subject of the verb. • In this sentence, "who" should be used because it is the subject of the clause "who was digging the ground," not the object. Corrected Sentence: "Who did you say was digging the ground?"

questions.question: Select the most appropriate SYNONYM of the given word. SURMOUNT
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Overcome. Explanation: The word "surmount" means to overcome or rise above something, especially obstacles or challenges. The synonym that closely matches this meaning is "overcome," which also refers to defeating or dealing with difficulties. Let’s see the meaning of all the words: • Surmount – पार करना – To overcome or rise above something, especially a difficulty or obstacle. Example: "She was able to surmount the challenges in her career and become successful." • Powerful – शक्तिशाली – Strong or having great power, not directly related to overcoming obstacles. Example: "He is a powerful speaker who can inspire others." • Ample – पर्याप्त – More than enough; abundant, which doesn’t relate to overcoming something. Example: "They had ample food for the party." • Precious – मूल्यवान – Something of great value or importance, unrelated to overcoming. Example: "Her precious jewellery was lost in the fire." • Overcome – पार करना – To succeed in dealing with or defeating something, especially challenges. Example: "He was able to overcome all his fears and give an excellent presentation."

questions.question: Select the most appropriate antonym of the underlined word in the following sentence- INORDINATE desire is at the root of all evils.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Reasonable. Explanation: The word "inordinate" means excessive or beyond what is considered reasonable or acceptable. Therefore, its antonym would be "reasonable," which refers to something that is fair, sensible, and within acceptable limits. Let’s see the meaning of all the words: • Inordinate – अत्यधिक – Excessive, more than what is necessary or appropriate. Example: "Her inordinate desire for power led to her downfall." • Outspoken – स्पष्टवादी – Someone who is frank, direct, or candid, not necessarily related to the idea of excess. Example: "The outspoken politician did not hesitate to share his views." • Concealed – छुपा हुआ – Hidden or not visible, unrelated to excess or reasonableness. Example: "The treasure was concealed deep in the cave." • Cheap – सस्ता – Low in cost, unrelated to the concept of desire or reasonableness in this context. Example: "The cheap materials used in construction resulted in poor quality." • Reasonable – उचित – Fair, sensible, within limits, and the opposite of excessive. Example: "He made a reasonable request for more time to finish the project."

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blanks. The archaeologist visited the _______ to get a better _______ of the ancient ruins.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Site, Sight. Explanation of all Homonyms: • Site - स्थल – A location or place, especially one where something important happens, such as an archaeological site or construction site. Example: "The archaeologist visited the site to examine the ancient ruins." • Sight - दृश्य – Refers to what can be seen or the act of seeing something. It refers to the visual appearance or view of something. Example: "The sight of the ancient ruins left the archaeologist in awe." • Cite - संदर्भ देना – To quote or refer to a source, often used in academic or legal contexts. Example: "The researcher will cite various studies in their report." Correct Answer Explanation: The sentence should read: "The archaeologist visited the site to get a better sight of the ancient ruins." This makes sense because the archaeologist is going to a site [a place or location] to observe or view the sight [the visual appearance] of the ruins.

questions.question: Select the word which means the same as the group of words. An animal or people who enjoy being in groups
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Gregarious. Explanation: • Herd - झुंड – A group of animals of the same species that live or move together. Example: "A herd of cattle grazed in the field." • Gregarious - सामाजिक – Describes animals or people who enjoy being in groups or are sociable. Example: "Parrots are gregarious birds and prefer to live in large flocks." • Egregious - अत्यंत बुरा – Outstandingly bad or shocking, usually referring to actions or behaviour. Example: "His egregious mistake cost the company millions." • Hoard - जमा करना – A large collection of things, often stored away, sometimes in secret. Example: "He has a hoard of antique coins hidden in his drawer." Therefore, Gregarious is correct as it specifically refers to animals or people who enjoy being in groups or are sociable.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom. I tried to attract his attention, but he was in a brown study.
options: A method of studying for an exam
A formal study of nature and the outdoors
A mood of deep absorption or thoughtfulness
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: A mood of deep absorption or thoughtfulness. Explanation: The idiom "in a brown study" refers to being lost in deep thought, often to the point of being unaware of one's surroundings. It describes a state of deep mental absorption, where a person is so focused on their thoughts or worries that they do not notice what is happening around them. Origin of the Idiom: The phrase "in a brown study" dates back to the 17th century, where "brown" referred to something sombre or dull in nature, indicating a mood of serious or introspective thought. The term "study" here does not mean academic study but rather contemplation or thinking deeply about something. Example: "I tried to attract his attention, but he was in a brown study, lost in his thoughts." Therefore, the correct meaning of the idiom "in a brown study" is "a mood of deep absorption or thoughtfulness."

questions.question: Select the word which means the same as the group of words given. Fear of Snakes
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Ophiophobia. Explanation of all Options: • Cynophobia - कुत्तों का भय - Fear of dogs. Example: "His cynophobia made it difficult for him to go to the park where dogs played." • Cacophobia - कुरूपता का भय- Fear of ugliness. Example: "Her cacophobia caused her to avoid looking at people she thought were unattractive." • Mysophobia - कीटाणुओं या गंदगी का भय - Fear of germs or dirt. Example: "His mysophobia led him to wash his hands repeatedly throughout the day." • Ophiophobia- सांपों का भय - Fear of snakes. Example: "Her ophiophobia made her avoid hiking in areas where snakes were common." Therefore, the correct word is Ophiophobia, as it specifically refers to the fear of snakes.

questions.question: Arrange the sentences in the correct order to form a meaningful paragraph. A. It should clearly present the purpose of the subject. B. Give the reader some information and tell him what subject you are going to deal with. C. Your opening paragraph should arrest the reader’s attention. D. Presentation is of very great importance in good writing.
questions.explanation: Correct Answer: DCAB Explanation: • D introduces the importance of presentation in writing: "Presentation is of very great importance in good writing." • C provides the next step: "Your opening paragraph should arrest the reader’s attention." • A emphasizes the clarity of purpose: "It should clearly present the purpose of the subject." • B gives further detail on what should follow in the paragraph: "Give the reader some information and tell him what subject you are going to deal with." Final Paragraph: Presentation is of very great importance in good writing. Your opening paragraph should arrest the reader’s attention. It should clearly present the purpose of the subject. Give the reader some information and tell him what subject you are going to deal with.

questions.question: Choose the most appropriate option to change the voice of the given sentence. Many people believe in magic.
options: Magic has been believed by many people.
Magic is believed in by many people.
Magic is believed by many people.
Magic has believed in many people.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Magic is believed in by many people. Explanation: The given sentence follows the active voice structure: Subject + Verb + Object → Many people believe in magic. To convert it into passive voice, we use the structure: Object + am/are/is + past participle + in + by + Subject. → Magic is believed in by many people. • The object of the sentence is "magic." • The verb "believe" changes to its past participle form "believed." • The preposition "in" stays with the verb, and the agent "many people" is introduced by "by."

questions.question: Change the narration of the given sentence. “Do you want some more books?” asked my friend.
options: My friend asked me if I want some more books.
My friend said to me if I wanted some more books.
My friend asked me whether I wanted some more books.
My friend asked me that I want some more books.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: My friend asked me whether I wanted some more books. Explanation: • The given sentence is in direct speech: “Do you want some more books?” asked my friend. • To convert it into indirect speech, we follow these rules: • The reporting verb "asked" remains the same because it’s a question. • The word "Do" in the question changes to the past tense ["wanted] to match the indirect speech tense. • The question form changes, and instead of using "do," we use "whether" or "if" in indirect speech. • The pronoun "you" changes to "I" because the speaker is referring to the person being spoken to [in this case, "me"].

questions.question: In a small hill town there lived a thief. He was very smart and before breaking ____1____ houses for theft he used to enquire about the lifestyles of the people staying in that house. Then at the dead of night he used to commit ____2____. Dhanpatrai was a moneylender living in the same town. He was ____3____ and never wasted money. He had a daughter named Laxmi. The thief knew that Dhanpatrai was rich and he had a lot of jewellery with him. Smartly the thief found out the location of the house and ____4____ the theft. One night, getting up to the roof of the house he started removing the roof-covering made of earthen tiles. The moneylender heard the sound and was sure that a thief was on the roof of the house. Now he had to create some ploy. He hit ____5____ a plan. “Laxmi, Laxmi” he called out to his daughter who was sleeping. “Laxmi, I hope our money-box and jewellery are kept in the ceiling.” he asked cleverly. Choose the most appropriate option to fill in the blank 1 -
questions.explanation: Explanation - The phrase "breaking into" is a common idiomatic expression that means to enter a place, typically a building or a house, by force or without permission, often with the intention of stealing or committing a crime. So, the correct sentence is: "He was very smart and before breaking into houses for theft he used to enquire about the lifestyles of the people staying in that house."

questions.question: In a small hill town there lived a thief. He was very smart and before breaking ____1____ houses for theft he used to enquire about the lifestyles of the people staying in that house. Then at the dead of night he used to commit ____2____. Dhanpatrai was a moneylender living in the same town. He was ____3____ and never wasted money. He had a daughter named Laxmi. The thief knew that Dhanpatrai was rich and he had a lot of jewellery with him. Smartly the thief found out the location of the house and ____4____ the theft. One night, getting up to the roof of the house he started removing the roof-covering made of earthen tiles. The moneylender heard the sound and was sure that a thief was on the roof of the house. Now he had to create some ploy. He hit ____5____ a plan. “Laxmi, Laxmi” he called out to his daughter who was sleeping. “Laxmi, I hope our money-box and jewellery are kept in the ceiling.” he asked cleverly. Choose the most appropriate option to fill in the blank 2 -
questions.explanation: Explanation – The correct answer is Option B: theft. Theft is the most suitable choice because it accurately describes the action of the thief, which is to steal or take someone else's property without permission. The phrase "commit theft" is also a common and idiomatic expression that fits perfectly in this context. The other options are incorrect because: Crime is too broad and doesn't specifically describe the action of stealing. Allegation refers to a claim or accusation, which doesn't fit the context of the thief actually committing the act. Mistake implies an unintentional error, whereas the thief is intentionally committing an act of theft.

questions.question: In a small hill town there lived a thief. He was very smart and before breaking ____1____ houses for theft he used to enquire about the lifestyles of the people staying in that house. Then at the dead of night he used to commit ____2____. Dhanpatrai was a moneylender living in the same town. He was ____3____ and never wasted money. He had a daughter named Laxmi. The thief knew that Dhanpatrai was rich and he had a lot of jewellery with him. Smartly the thief found out the location of the house and ____4____ the theft. One night, getting up to the roof of the house he started removing the roof-covering made of earthen tiles. The moneylender heard the sound and was sure that a thief was on the roof of the house. Now he had to create some ploy. He hit ____5____ a plan. “Laxmi, Laxmi” he called out to his daughter who was sleeping. “Laxmi, I hope our money-box and jewellery are kept in the ceiling.” he asked cleverly. Choose the most appropriate option to fill in the blank 3 -
questions.explanation: Explanation – The correct answer is Option D: thrifty. Thrifty is the most suitable choice because it accurately describes someone who is careful with money and avoids unnecessary expenses, which fits perfectly with the context of Dhanpatrai never wasting money. The other options are incorrect because: Luxurious, lavish, and extravagant all imply excessive spending or a love for luxury, which is the opposite of Dhanpatrai's behavior.

questions.question: In a small hill town there lived a thief. He was very smart and before breaking ____1____ houses for theft he used to enquire about the lifestyles of the people staying in that house. Then at the dead of night he used to commit ____2____. Dhanpatrai was a moneylender living in the same town. He was ____3____ and never wasted money. He had a daughter named Laxmi. The thief knew that Dhanpatrai was rich and he had a lot of jewellery with him. Smartly the thief found out the location of the house and ____4____ the theft. One night, getting up to the roof of the house he started removing the roof-covering made of earthen tiles. The moneylender heard the sound and was sure that a thief was on the roof of the house. Now he had to create some ploy. He hit ____5____ a plan. “Laxmi, Laxmi” he called out to his daughter who was sleeping. “Laxmi, I hope our money-box and jewellery are kept in the ceiling.” he asked cleverly. Choose the most appropriate option to fill in the blank 4 -
questions.explanation: Explanation – The correct answer is Option C: designed. Designed is the most suitable choice because it implies that the thief carefully planned and strategized the theft, which fits perfectly with the context of the thief being smart and finding out the location of the house. The other options are incorrect because: Committed implies carrying out the act, which doesn't fit the context of planning. Initiated might imply starting the process, but designed fits better with the idea of planning and strategizing. Avoided means staying away from, which is the opposite of what the thief intended to do.

questions.question: In a small hill town there lived a thief. He was very smart and before breaking ____1____ houses for theft he used to enquire about the lifestyles of the people staying in that house. Then at the dead of night he used to commit ____2____. Dhanpatrai was a moneylender living in the same town. He was ____3____ and never wasted money. He had a daughter named Laxmi. The thief knew that Dhanpatrai was rich and he had a lot of jewellery with him. Smartly the thief found out the location of the house and ____4____ the theft. One night, getting up to the roof of the house he started removing the roof-covering made of earthen tiles. The moneylender heard the sound and was sure that a thief was on the roof of the house. Now he had to create some ploy. He hit ____5____ a plan. “Laxmi, Laxmi” he called out to his daughter who was sleeping. “Laxmi, I hope our money-box and jewellery are kept in the ceiling.” he asked cleverly. Choose the most appropriate option to fill in the blank 5 -
questions.explanation: Explanation – The correct answer is Option A: upon. Upon is the most suitable choice because it fits perfectly with the phrase "hit upon a plan", which is an idiomatic expression meaning to come up with or devise a plan. The other options are incorrect because: Onto implies movement or direction, which doesn't fit the context. Up to implies a limit or extent, which doesn't fit the context. Into implies entering or moving into something, which doesn't fit the context of devising a plan.
